questions.prompt: What should a good HEALTHCHECK endpoint do?

choices.text: Quick verification of real readiness — hit a cheap endpoint that confirms the process is responsive and key dependencies are reachable (DB connection alive, downstream service responding). Keep it fast (<1s). Don't query the DB on every check (cascading load). Return 200 only when actually ready to serve traffic. Return non-zero/non-200 during startup or when degraded.
Just check the port is open.

questions.explanation: Healthcheck = cheap, fast, reflects real readiness. Not just process-alive; not heavy validation.

questions.prompt: A container restart loops every 30 seconds with `--restart always`. What's happening?

choices.text: App crashes immediately on startup; restart policy relaunches it; same crash; loop. Docker applies exponential backoff (10s, 20s, 40s, capped), but eventually the container is marked dead. Restart policy doesn't fix bad apps — it handles transient failures. Diagnostic flow: `docker logs container` to see why startup fails (usually missing config, dependency, env var, file). Fix the root cause; don't just keep restarting.

questions.explanation: Restart loops indicate app issues, not infrastructure. Check logs; fix the app.
